# Hook: PreAgentHandoff

> **Status**: 🚧 WIP — Planned for v2.29.0  
> **Lifecycle Event**: SessionStart when resuming work on an existing plan or branch  
> **Depends On**: v2.27.0 (all LiveGuard tools), v2.28.0 (dashboard + telemetry)  
> **Blocks**: Nothing — context injection only

---

## Purpose

Bridge the context gap between Plan Forge's 4-session model and the Claude Co-work / multi-agent workflow. When a new agent session opens on an active plan, it starts blind to post-ship health. This hook injects LiveGuard's current state as structured context at session start — before the agent reads any plan files, writes any code, or runs any commands.

Also serves as the **OpenClaw bridge event source**: after injecting context into the agent session, optionally POSTs the same state snapshot to OpenClaw's configured endpoint so it can maintain a cross-project health timeline without polling.

---

## Trigger Conditions

Fire this hook on `SessionStart` when **any** of the following are true:

1. A plan file exists at `docs/plans/*.md` matching files modified in the current git branch (dirty branch detection via `git diff --name-only origin/master`)
2. A EXECUTE-PHASE prompt is loaded (file path matches `*EXECUTE*.prompt.md` or `*step3*.prompt.md`)
3. A `docs/plans/auto/LIVEGUARD-FIX-*.md` auto-fix plan exists (generated by `forge_fix_proposal`)
4. The loaded session references a `--resume-from` flag or slice index in its prompt header

---

## Actions

### 1. LiveGuard State Snapshot

Run the following in sequence (fast read-only operations — no subprocesses unless cache is stale):

```bash
pforge triage --min-severity medium --max 10
pforge drift
```

Read from caches (no rerun if cache age < 30 minutes):
- `.forge/alert-triage-cache.json` → ranked alerts
- `.forge/drift-history.json` → last score + trend
- `.forge/incidents/` → any open incidents (absent `resolvedAt`)
- `.forge/secret-scan-cache.json` → last scan result + age
- `.forge/deploy-journal.jsonl` → last deploy entry

### 2. Inject Context Header

Prepend the following structured block to the agent's context as a system message, before any user prompt content:

```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
🛡️ LIVEGUARD CONTEXT — Session Start
(As of {snapshotAge} ago — run `pforge triage` to refresh)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Drift Score: {score}/100 ({trend}) — {violations.length} active violations
Open Incidents: {openIncidents.length} ({openIncidents.map(i => i.severity).join(', ')})
Last Deploy: {lastDeploy.version} @ {lastDeploy.timestamp} (pre: {lastDeploy.preHealthScore}, post: {lastDeploy.postHealthScore ?? 'not yet recorded'})
Last Secret Scan: {secretScan.clean ? '✅ Clean' : `⛔ ${secretScan.findings.length} finding(s)`} ({secretScanAge})

Top Alerts (medium+):
{alerts.slice(0,5).map((a, i) =>
  `${i+1}. [${a.severity.toUpperCase()}] ${a.title} — ${a.recommendedAction}`
).join('\n')}

{alerts.length > 5 ? `...and ${alerts.length - 5} more. Run \`pforge triage\` for full list.` : ''}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

If all LiveGuard data stores are empty (v2.27 not yet invoked on this project):

```
🛡️ LIVEGUARD CONTEXT — No data yet
Run `pforge triage` after completing the first deploy to activate LiveGuard monitoring.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

### 3. Regression Guard on Dirty Branch (SessionResume path)

If trigger condition #1 fired (dirty branch with modified files vs. master):

```bash
pforge regression-guard --files <git-diff-files>
```

If any gates fail, append to the context header:

```
⚠️ Regression Alert — {failed} gate(s) failing on current branch changes

{results.filter(r => r.status === 'failed').map(r =>
  `• Slice ${r.sliceId} (${r.planFile}): ${r.command}`
).join('\n')}

Resolve these before adding new code — the current branch has introduced regressions.
```

### 4. OpenClaw Bridge Event (PostAnalyze)

If `openclaw.endpoint` is configured in `.forge.json`:

```js
POST {openclaw.endpoint}/api/liveguard/snapshot
Authorization: Bearer {openclaw.apiKey}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "project": "<repo-name from git remote>",
  "timestamp": "<ISO>",
  "driftScore": score,
  "driftTrend": trend,
  "openIncidents": openIncidents.length,
  "lastDeployVersion": lastDeploy.version,
  "alertSummary": { "critical": N, "high": N, "medium": N },
  "secretScanClean": secretScan.clean
}
```

This POST is fire-and-forget (non-blocking): if OpenClaw is unreachable, log `"openclaw snapshot skipped: <error>"` to the session and continue. Never throw from a failed OpenClaw POST.

OpenClaw uses these snapshots to build a cross-project health timeline — the same data the LiveGuard dashboard shows per-project, but aggregated across all projects in the OpenClaw workspace.

---

## Configuration (`.forge.json`)

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "preAgentHandoff": {
      "enabled": true,
      "injectContext": true,
      "runRegressionGuard": true,
      "cacheMaxAgeMinutes": 30,
      "minAlertSeverity": "medium"
    }
  },
  "openclaw": {
    "endpoint": "https://your-openclaw-instance/",
    "apiKey": "<set in .forge/secrets.json>"
  }
}
```

The `openclaw` block is optional. If absent, step 4 (PostAnalyze bridge) is skipped silently.

---

## Session Handoff Checklist (injected as reminder at session end)

When the `Stop` hook fires at the end of a session where this hook ran, append:

```
🛡️ LiveGuard Session Handoff Reminder

Before closing this session:
□ Run `pforge triage` for an updated alert snapshot
□ Run `pforge deploy-log "<version>" --by "agent"` if a deploy occurred
□ Run `pforge incident "<description>"` for any unresolved errors encountered
□ The next session will receive this LiveGuard context automatically on start
```

---

## Non-Goals

- Does NOT make decisions about whether to proceed with the session — context injection only
- Does NOT modify any plan files or code based on LiveGuard state
- Does NOT create branches or commits
- Does NOT poll or run in the background — fires once on SessionStart, done
- Does NOT replace reading the plan file — injects LiveGuard context *before* the plan is loaded, so the agent reads the plan already aware of current health

---

## Implementation Notes (for v2.29.0 Slice)

- Wire into the existing `SessionStart` hook mechanism in `.github/hooks/`
- All 4 read operations (triage + drift + incidents + deploy-journal) are file reads against `.forge/` — no subprocesses unless cache is stale. Total injection latency target: <500ms
- If `bridge.approvalSecret` is configured, the OpenClaw POST should include `X-Forge-Secret` header for endpoint auth
- The context header injected here is recognized by the agent's system prompt and displayed visually distinct from user instructions — use the `━━━` separator pattern established in the status-reporting instructions for visual consistency
- `preAgentHandoff.cacheMaxAgeMinutes` controls when a fresh subprocess run is triggered vs. reading from cache files
